title: Ochna magdalenae
intro: Ochna magdalenae, also known as the Cape bird's eye, is a small shrub native to South Africa, belonging to the family Ochnaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its purported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Ochna magdalenae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Ochna magdalenae is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in the production of capsules and infusions to harness its potential health benefits.
taxonomy: Ochna magdalenae is classified within the family Ochnaceae, a group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Ochna is a member of the order Malpighiales, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and genetic characteristics, which are distinct from those of other flowering plant families. Within the Ochnaceae family, Ochna magdalenae is further classified as a species within the genus Ochna. This classification helps to identify and distinguish Ochna magdalenae from other similar species within the same family.

In traditional medicine, the plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
